title: Colors, Fills, and Lines
text: Use MSO_THEME_COLOR value apply color follows presentation theme: Apply tint (lighten toward white) shade (darken toward black):
code: r <- blank_slide(); slide <- r$slide shape <- new_rect(slide)  shape$fill$solid() shape$fill$fore_color$theme_color <- MSO_THEME_COLOR$ACCENT_1 shape$fill$fore_color$type   # "scheme" #> [1] "scheme" r <- blank_slide(); slide <- r$slide shape <- new_rect(slide)  shape$fill$solid() shape$fill$fore_color$theme_color <- MSO_THEME_COLOR$ACCENT_1 shape$fill$fore_color$tint  <- 0.4   # 40 % lighter shape$fill$fore_color$shade <- NULL  # remove shade (if any)

title: Colors, Fills, and Lines
code: r <- blank_slide(); slide <- r$slide shape <- new_rect(slide) shape$fill$background() shape$fill$type   # "background" #> NULL

title: Colors, Fills, and Lines
text: fill$gradient() creates default two-stop linear gradient. Customise stops angle afterwards:
code: r <- blank_slide(); slide <- r$slide shape <- new_rect(slide)  shape$fill$gradient() shape$fill$gradient_angle <- 270.0   # top-to-bottom  stops <- shape$fill$gradient_stops stops[[1]]$color$rgb <- RGBColor(0x4F, 0x81, 0xBD)  # start color stops[[2]]$color$rgb <- RGBColor(0xBD, 0xD7, 0xEE)  # end color  length(stops)               # 2 #> [1] 2 stops[[1]]$position         # 0 #> [1] 0 stops[[2]]$position         # 1 #> [1] 1

title: Colors, Fills, and Lines
text: fill$patterned() creates hatched pattern. Set preset fill$pattern control foreground/background colors fore_color/back_color:
code: r <- blank_slide(); slide <- r$slide shape <- new_rect(slide)  shape$fill$patterned() shape$fill$pattern <- MSO_PATTERN_TYPE$DIAGONAL_BRICK shape$fill$fore_color$rgb <- RGBColor(0x4F, 0x81, 0xBD) shape$fill$back_color$rgb <- RGBColor(0xFF, 0xFF, 0xFF)  shape$fill$type     # "patterned" #> [1] "patterned" shape$fill$pattern  # "diagBrick" #> [1] "diagBrick"

title: Colors, Fills, and Lines

what: Putting it together: a styled shape
title: Colors, Fills, and Lines
code: r <- blank_slide() prs   <- r$prs slide <- r$slide  shape <- slide$shapes$add_shape(   MSO_AUTO_SHAPE_TYPE$ROUNDED_RECTANGLE,   Inches(1), Inches(1), Inches(4), Inches(2) )  # Gradient fill: blue to sky-blue shape$fill$gradient() shape$fill$gradient_angle <- 135.0 shape$fill$gradient_stops[[1]]$color$rgb <- RGBColor(0x00, 0x70, 0xC0) shape$fill$gradient_stops[[2]]$color$rgb <- RGBColor(0x9D, 0xC3, 0xE6)  # White border shape$line$width     <- Pt(1.5) shape$line$color$rgb <- RGBColor(0xFF, 0xFF, 0xFF)  # Text tf  <- shape$text_frame run <- tf$paragraphs[[1]]$add_run() run$text <- "Styled shape" tf$paragraphs[[1]]$runs[[1]]$font$color$rgb <- RGBColor(0xFF, 0xFF, 0xFF) tf$paragraphs[[1]]$runs[[1]]$font$bold      <- TRUE  tmp <- tempfile(fileext = ".pptx") prs$save(tmp)

title: Getting Started with rpptx
text: pptx_presentation() opens existing file ŌĆö argument ŌĆö creates new blank presentation based built-default template. Check slide dimensions (EMU ŌĆö English Metric Units, 914400 per inch): Use Inches(), Cm(), Pt() convert convenient units EMU:
code: prs <- pptx_presentation() prs$slide_width   # 10 inches #> <Length: 9144000 EMU (10.00 in, 25.40 cm, 720.0 pt)> prs$slide_height  # 7.5 inches #> <Length: 6858000 EMU (7.50 in, 19.05 cm, 540.0 pt)> Inches(1)     # 914400L #> <Length: 914400 EMU (1.00 in, 2.54 cm, 72.0 pt)> Cm(2.54)      # 914400L #> <Length: 914400 EMU (1.00 in, 2.54 cm, 72.0 pt)> Pt(72)        # 914400L #> <Length: 914400 EMU (1.00 in, 2.54 cm, 72.0 pt)>

title: Getting Started with rpptx
text: Slides added Slides collection using slide layout template. Every new blank presentation one slide master eleven layouts. Add blank slide (layout index 6 ŌĆ£BlankŌĆØ default template): Access individual slides 1-based index:
code: # List available layout names sapply(seq_len(length(prs$slide_layouts)), function(i) {   prs$slide_layouts[[i]]$name }) #>  [1] "Title Slide"             "Title and Content"       #>  [3] "Section Header"          "Two Content"             #>  [5] "Comparison"              "Title Only"              #>  [7] "Blank"                   "Content with Caption"    #>  [9] "Picture with Caption"    "Title and Vertical Text" #> [11] "Vertical Title and Text" layout <- prs$slide_layouts[[6]] slide  <- prs$slides$add_slide(layout) length(prs$slides)    # 1 #> [1] 1 prs$slides[[1]]       # the slide we just added #> <Slide>  1 shape #>   Placeholder     Title                     9.00├Ś1.25 in @ 0.50, 0.30

title: Working with Shapes
Working with Shapes
text: shapes share properties: Positions sizes read/write:
code: r <- blank_slide() slide <- r$slide shape <- slide$shapes$add_shape(   MSO_AUTO_SHAPE_TYPE$RECTANGLE,   left = Inches(1), top = Inches(1),   width = Inches(3), height = Inches(2) )  shape$name         # "Rectangle 1" #> [1] "AutoShape 2" shape$shape_id     # integer ID unique within the slide #> [1] 3 shape$left         # position in EMU #> <Length: 914400 EMU (1.00 in, 2.54 cm, 72.0 pt)> shape$top #> <Length: 914400 EMU (1.00 in, 2.54 cm, 72.0 pt)> shape$width #> <Length: 2743200 EMU (3.00 in, 7.62 cm, 216.0 pt)> shape$height #> <Length: 1828800 EMU (2.00 in, 5.08 cm, 144.0 pt)> shape$shape_type   # MSO_SHAPE_TYPE$AUTO_SHAPE #> [1] 1 shape$left   <- Inches(2) shape$top    <- Inches(3) shape$width  <- Inches(4) shape$height <- Inches(1.5)

title: Working with Shapes
text: add_shape() accepts MSO_AUTO_SHAPE_TYPE constant: commonly used shape types:
code: r <- blank_slide() slide <- r$slide  rect  <- slide$shapes$add_shape(MSO_AUTO_SHAPE_TYPE$RECTANGLE,            Inches(0.5), Inches(0.5), Inches(2), Inches(1.5)) oval  <- slide$shapes$add_shape(MSO_AUTO_SHAPE_TYPE$OVAL,            Inches(3),   Inches(0.5), Inches(2), Inches(1.5)) arrow <- slide$shapes$add_shape(MSO_AUTO_SHAPE_TYPE$RIGHT_ARROW,            Inches(5.5), Inches(0.5), Inches(3), Inches(1.5)) MSO_AUTO_SHAPE_TYPE$RECTANGLE #> $value #> [1] 1 #>  #> $prst #> [1] "rect" MSO_AUTO_SHAPE_TYPE$ROUNDED_RECTANGLE #> $value #> [1] 5 #>  #> $prst #> [1] "roundRect" MSO_AUTO_SHAPE_TYPE$OVAL #> $value #> [1] 9 #>  #> $prst #> [1] "ellipse" MSO_AUTO_SHAPE_TYPE$DIAMOND #> $value #> [1] 4 #>  #> $prst #> [1] "diamond" MSO_AUTO_SHAPE_TYPE$TRIANGLE #> NULL MSO_AUTO_SHAPE_TYPE$RIGHT_ARROW #> $value #> [1] 33 #>  #> $prst #> [1] "rightArrow" MSO_AUTO_SHAPE_TYPE$STAR_5_POINT #> $value #> [1] 92 #>  #> $prst #> [1] "star5" MSO_AUTO_SHAPE_TYPE$CLOUD #> $value #> [1] 179 #>  #> $prst #> [1] "cloud"

title: Working with Shapes
text: Placeholder shapes (slide layout) accessed via placeholders: Set placeholder text: ([[n]] SlidePlaceholders accepts 1-based position, OOXML idx.)
code: prs    <- pptx_presentation() layout <- prs$slide_layouts[[1]]   # "Title Slide" layout slide  <- prs$slides$add_slide(layout)  # List placeholder indices and types for (ph in slide$placeholders$to_list()) {   cat(ph$placeholder_format$idx, "ŌĆö", ph$placeholder_format$type, "\n") } #> 0 ŌĆö ctrTitle  #> 1 ŌĆö subTitle ph1 <- slide$placeholders[[1]]; ph1$text <- "My Title" ph2 <- slide$placeholders[[2]]; ph2$text <- "My Subtitle"

title: Working with Shapes
text: Embed video file slide add_movie(). shape shows poster frame image video plays. default poster frame generic loudspeaker icon bundled package. Supply image poster_frame_image: video relationship uses OOXML dual-relationship pattern required Office 2007/2010 compatibility: one RT.MEDIA relationship one RT.VIDEO relationship pointing media part. Multiple calls file reuse single media part (SHA-1 deduplication).
code: mp4 <- system.file("test_files", "dummy.mp4", package = "rpptx")  r     <- blank_slide() slide <- r$slide  shape <- slide$shapes$add_movie(   movie_file = mp4,   left = Inches(1), top = Inches(1),   width = Inches(6), height = Inches(4) ) shape$shape_type  # MSO_SHAPE_TYPE$PICTURE img <- system.file("test_files", "monty-truth.png", package = "rpptx") slide$shapes$add_movie(mp4,   Inches(1), Inches(1), Inches(6), Inches(4),   poster_frame_image = img,   mime_type = "video/mp4" )

what: Filling a table from a data frame
title: Working with Tables
code: r <- blank_slide() slide <- r$slide  df <- data.frame(   Name    = c("Alice", "Bob", "Carol"),   Score   = c(92, 85, 78),   Grade   = c("A", "B+", "B"),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE )  nr   <- nrow(df) + 1L   # +1 for header nc   <- ncol(df)  tbl_shape <- slide$shapes$add_table(   nr, nc,   Inches(1), Inches(1), Inches(8), Inches(3.5) ) tbl <- tbl_shape$table  # Write column headers for (j in seq_len(nc)) {   cell <- tbl$cell(1, j)   cell$text <- names(df)[j] }  # Write data rows for (i in seq_len(nrow(df))) {   for (j in seq_len(nc)) {     cell <- tbl$cell(i + 1L, j)     cell$text <- as.character(df[i, j])   } }

title: Working with Tables
code: r <- blank_slide() slide <- r$slide tbl_shape <- slide$shapes$add_table(3, 3, Inches(1), Inches(1), Inches(7), Inches(3)) tbl <- tbl_shape$table  # Blue header row for (j in 1:3) {   cell <- tbl$cell(1, j)   cell$fill$solid()   cell$fill$fore_color$rgb <- RGBColor(0x4F, 0x81, 0xBD) }  # Alternating row shading for (i in 2:3) {   for (j in 1:3) {     cell <- tbl$cell(i, j)     if (i %% 2 == 0) {       cell$fill$solid()       cell$fill$fore_color$rgb <- RGBColor(0xDB, 0xE5, 0xF1)     } else {       cell$fill$background()     }   } }

title: Working with Tables
code: prs    <- pptx_presentation() layout <- prs$slide_layouts[[6]] slide  <- prs$slides$add_slide(layout)  # Sample data sales <- data.frame(   Region  = c("North", "South", "East", "West", "Total"),   Q1      = c(234, 187, 312, 145, 878),   Q2      = c(256, 201, 298, 167, 922),   Q3      = c(289, 223, 341, 189, 1042),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE )  nr <- nrow(sales) + 1L nc <- ncol(sales)  ts <- slide$shapes$add_table(   nr, nc,   Inches(0.5), Inches(0.5),   Inches(9),   Inches(5) ) tbl <- ts$table  # Headers header_color <- RGBColor(0x17, 0x37, 0x5E) for (j in seq_len(nc)) {   cell <- tbl$cell(1, j)   cell$text <- names(sales)[j]   cell$fill$solid()   cell$fill$fore_color$rgb <- header_color   tf <- cell$text_frame   tf$paragraphs[[1]]$font$bold  <- TRUE   tf$paragraphs[[1]]$font$color$rgb <- RGBColor(0xFF, 0xFF, 0xFF) }  # Data rows for (i in seq_len(nrow(sales))) {   row_color <- if (i %% 2 == 1) RGBColor(0xD6, 0xDC, 0xE4) else RGBColor(0xFF, 0xFF, 0xFF)   is_total  <- sales$Region[i] == "Total"   for (j in seq_len(nc)) {     cell <- tbl$cell(i + 1L, j)     cell$text <- as.character(sales[i, j])     cell$fill$solid()     cell$fill$fore_color$rgb <- if (is_total) header_color else row_color     if (is_total) {       cell$text_frame$paragraphs[[1]]$font$bold  <- TRUE       cell$text_frame$paragraphs[[1]]$font$color$rgb <- RGBColor(0xFF, 0xFF, 0xFF)     }     if (j > 1) {       cell$text_frame$paragraphs[[1]]$alignment <- PP_PARAGRAPH_ALIGNMENT$RIGHT     }   } }  # Column widths tbl$columns[[1]]$width <- Inches(2) for (j in 2:nc) tbl$columns[[j]]$width <- Inches(7 / (nc - 1))  tmp <- tempfile(fileext = ".pptx") prs$save(tmp)

code: shape <- shape_with_text() shape$text <- "Hello world" shape$text #> [1] "Hello world" shape$text_frame$text <- "Updated text" shape$text_frame$text #> [1] "Updated text"

title: Working with Text
text: Paragraph alignment: Alignment values: LEFT, CENTER, RIGHT, JUSTIFY, DISTRIBUTE. Indentation level (0ŌĆō8): Line paragraph spacing (points):
code: shape <- shape_with_text() tf    <- shape$text_frame  # Add paragraphs p1 <- tf$add_paragraph() p1$text <- "First paragraph"  p2 <- tf$add_paragraph() p2$text <- "Second paragraph"  length(tf$paragraphs)  # 2 (there's always a default empty paragraph first) #> [1] 3 p1$alignment <- PP_PARAGRAPH_ALIGNMENT$CENTER p2$alignment <- PP_PARAGRAPH_ALIGNMENT$RIGHT p2$level <- 1L   # indent one level p1$line_spacing  <- 1.5    # 1.5├Ś line height p1$space_before  <- Pt(6) p1$space_after   <- Pt(12)

code: prs    <- pptx_presentation() layout <- prs$slide_layouts[[6]] slide  <- prs$slides$add_slide(layout)  tb <- slide$shapes$add_textbox(   Inches(1), Inches(1), Inches(8), Inches(5.5) ) tf <- tb$text_frame tf$word_wrap <- TRUE  # First paragraph ŌĆö heading style tf$clear() p1 <- tf$paragraphs[[1]] p1$text <- "rpptx Text Demo" p1$font$bold <- TRUE p1$font$size <- Pt(28) p1$alignment <- PP_PARAGRAPH_ALIGNMENT$CENTER  # Second paragraph ŌĆö normal text with mixed formatting p2 <- tf$add_paragraph() p2$space_before <- Pt(12) r1 <- p2$add_run(); r1$text <- "You can mix " r2 <- p2$add_run(); r2$text <- "bold"; r2$font$bold <- TRUE r3 <- p2$add_run(); r3$text <- ", " r4 <- p2$add_run(); r4$text <- "italic"; r4$font$italic <- TRUE r5 <- p2$add_run(); r5$text <- ", and " r6 <- p2$add_run() r6$text <- "coloured" r6$font$color$rgb <- RGBColor(0xC0, 0x00, 0x00) r7 <- p2$add_run(); r7$text <- " text freely."  # Third paragraph ŌĆö bulleted list simulation bullets <- c("First item", "Second item", "Third item") for (b in bullets) {   p <- tf$add_paragraph()   p$text  <- b   p$level <- 1L   p$space_before <- Pt(4) }  tmp <- tempfile(fileext = ".pptx") prs$save(tmp)
